content.text: The system shall provide an Inspection Findings screen accessible from the navigation menu under the MHE module.
access the Inspection Findings screen from the MHE navigation menu
I can review inspection-related findings and records.
the user is within the application navigation menu
the user navigates to the MHE module and selects Inspection Findings
the system opens the Inspection Findings screen.
The system shall display the page title as “Inspection Findings” at the top of the screen.
the page title displayed at the top of the screen
I can identify the current module and page.
the Inspection Findings screen is displayed
the system displays the page title as “Inspection Findings”.
The system shall display a searchable findings table containing inspection finding records.
I can review inspection finding records efficiently.
the Inspection Findings screen is displayed
the system displays a searchable findings table containing inspection finding records.
The system shall display a search input field with placeholder text “Search findings…” above the findings table.
a search input field displayed above the findings table
I can search inspection findings quickly.
the Inspection Findings screen is displayed
the system displays a search input field above the findings table
the field displays the placeholder text “Search findings…”.
The system shall allow the user to search inspection findings using keywords entered in the search field.
I can quickly locate relevant records.
the search input field is displayed
the user enters keywords into the search field
the system searches inspection findings using the entered keywords.

6.title: Dynamically Filter Findings Table Based on Search

content.text: The system shall dynamically filter table records based on the entered search criteria.
the findings table filtered dynamically based on search criteria
I can view only matching inspection records.
the user enters or updates search criteria
the system dynamically filters table records based on the entered search criteria.
The system shall display a Severity filter dropdown above the findings table.
a Severity filter dropdown displayed above the findings table

the Inspection Findings screen is displayed
the system displays a Severity filter dropdown above the findings table.
The system shall display the default Severity filter option as “All Severity”.
the default Severity filter option displayed as “All Severity”
I can understand that all severity levels are currently included.
the Severity filter dropdown is displayed
the screen loads for the first time
the system displays the default Severity filter option as “All Severity”.
The system shall allow the user to filter inspection findings based on severity categories.
filter inspection findings by severity category
I can focus on findings of a specific severity level.
the Severity filter dropdown is displayed
the user selects a severity category
the system filters inspection findings based on the selected severity category.
The system shall display a Status filter dropdown above the findings table.
a Status filter dropdown displayed above the findings table
I can filter inspection findings by status.
the Inspection Findings screen is displayed
the system displays a Status filter dropdown above the findings table.
The system shall display the default Status filter option as “All Status”.
the default Status filter option displayed as “All Status”
I can understand that findings of all statuses are currently shown.
the Status filter dropdown is displayed
the system displays the default Status filter option as “All Status”.
The system shall allow the user to filter inspection findings based on status values.
filter inspection findings based on status values
I can view findings matching a selected workflow state.
the Status filter dropdown is displayed
the user selects a status value
the system filters inspection findings based on the selected status value.
The system shall display the inspection findings in a tabular layout.
inspection findings displayed in a table layout
I can review records in a structured format.
the Inspection Findings screen is displayed
the system displays inspection findings in a tabular layout.
The system shall display the following columns within the findings table: Finding ID, MHE Name, Part Name, Checklist, Reported By, Severity, Status, and Created At.
relevant columns displayed in the findings table
I can review complete inspection finding information.
the system displays the following columns: Finding ID, MHE Name, Part Name, Checklist, Reported By, Severity, Status, and Created At.
The system shall display the Finding ID column values as clickable links for navigation to detailed finding information.
Finding ID values displayed as clickable links
I can navigate to detailed finding information.
the system displays Finding ID column values as clickable links
clicking a Finding ID navigates the user to the detailed finding view.

16.title: Display MHE Name for Each Finding

content.text: The system shall display the MHE Name associated with each inspection finding record.
the MHE Name displayed for each inspection finding
I can identify the related equipment.
the system displays the associated MHE Name for each inspection finding record.

17.title: Display Related Part Name for Each Finding

content.text: The system shall display the related Part Name for each inspection finding record.
the related Part Name displayed for each inspection finding
I can identify the affected component.
the system displays the related Part Name for each inspection finding record.

18.title: Display Checklist Reference for Each Finding

content.text: The system shall display the related Checklist reference for each inspection finding record.
the related Checklist reference displayed for each inspection finding

The system shall display the name of the user who reported the finding under the Reported By column.

20.title: Display Severity Using Coloured Status Badges

content.text: The system shall display the finding severity using severity-specific colored status badges including Red, Amber, and Green.
severity levels displayed using coloured status badges
I can quickly identify the severity of inspection findings.
the system displays severity values using severity-specific coloured status badges including Red, Amber, and Green.

21.title: Differentiate Severity Badges Using Colour Coding

content.text: The system shall visually differentiate severity badges using severity-specific colour coding.
severity badges visually differentiated using colour coding

severity badges are displayed in the findings table
the system visually differentiates severity badges using severity-specific colour coding.

22.title: Display Finding Status Using Status Badges

inspection finding statuses displayed using status badges

The system shall visually differentiate finding statuses using distinct badge styling.
finding statuses visually differentiated using distinct badge styles

status badges are displayed in the findings table

The system shall display the finding creation timestamp under the Created At column.

the system displays the finding creation timestamp under the Created At column.

25.title: Support Vertical Scrolling in Findings Table

28.title: Display Current Record Range and Total Count

the current record range and total record count displayed

29.title: Provide Previous and Next Pagination Controls

53.title: Display Timeline Events in Chronological Layout

content.text: The system shall display inspection activity records in a vertical chronological timeline layout.
inspection activity records displayed in a vertical chronological timeline

the system displays inspection activity records in a vertical chronological timeline layout.
The system shall display a timeline connector line linking all timeline events sequentially.
a connector line linking timeline events

the system displays a timeline connector line linking all timeline events sequentially.
The system shall display circular timeline markers for each inspection timeline event.
circular markers displayed for each timeline event
I can visually distinguish individual events.
the system displays circular timeline markers for each inspection timeline event.

56.title: Differentiate Active or Latest Timeline Event

content.text: The system shall visually differentiate the currently active or latest timeline event marker from previous events.
the latest or active timeline event visually differentiated

the system visually differentiates the currently active or latest timeline event marker from previous events.
The system shall display the event severity label for each timeline entry in the format “Severity: ”.
the event severity displayed for each timeline entry
I can identify the severity level associated with timeline events.
the system displays the event severity label for each timeline entry in the format “Severity:”.

58.title: Support Multiple Severity Values in Timeline

content.text: The system shall support multiple severity values including Red and Amber within the timeline entries.
multiple severity values supported in timeline entries
I can distinguish different severity levels within the timeline.
the system supports multiple severity values including Red and Amber within the timeline entries.

59.title: Display Reporting Entity Using By Label

content.text: The system shall display the reporting entity or user below each timeline event using the “By” label.
the reporting entity displayed below each timeline event

the system displays the reporting entity or user below each timeline event using the “By” label.
The system shall display the associated user or source identifier for each timeline event.
the associated user or source identifier displayed for each event

the system displays the associated user or source identifier for each timeline event.
The system shall display the event date and timestamp aligned with each timeline entry.
the event date and timestamp displayed with each timeline entry

the system displays the event date and timestamp aligned with each timeline entry.

62.title: Display Timeline in Descending Chronological Order

content.text: The system shall display timeline entries in descending chronological order, with the latest event appearing first.
the latest timeline event displayed first
I can review the most recent inspection activity immediately.
the system displays timeline entries in descending chronological order with the latest event appearing first.
The system shall visually separate timeline entries using divider lines or spacing for readability.
I can read and distinguish events more clearly.
the system visually separates timeline entries using divider lines or spacing for readability.
The system shall dynamically render timeline events based on available inspection history data.
timeline events rendered dynamically from inspection history data

the system dynamically renders timeline events based on the available inspection history data.

65.title: Preserve Historical Event Order and Severity

content.text: The system shall preserve the order and severity information of all historical inspection events within the timeline.
the order and severity information of historical events preserved
I can accurately review the inspection history timeline.
the system preserves the order and severity information of all historical inspection events within the timeline.

The system shall maintain consistent alignment between timeline markers, event details, and timestamps across all timeline entries.
